supporting_quotes: We introduce a large language model (LLM) capable of processing speech inputs and show that tuning it further with reinforcement learning on human preference (RLHF) enables it to adapt better to disordered speech than traditional fine-tuning. (Abstract)

Our eventual goal is to adapt the LLM based ASR model to recognize disordered speech... we explore tuning the model with a reward from two sources of feedback, (1) WER between the transcript and ground truth... and (2) Meaning Preservation (MP), an LLM-based semantic measure of correctness. (Section IV, Domain Adaptation with RLHF)
